description:
GOAL: Optimize hot calls by fusing adjacent frames into one Cranelift fn — no
allocation for fused calls. Performance-load-bearing for v1.

## Decisions captured

- IN SCOPE for v1, NOT deferred (user decision: "i think we should inline").
  Naive frame-per-call is too slow for tight loops.
- Operate on fz-IR BEFORE Cranelift lowering. Module-level pass: in-place
  rewrites of caller FnIrs. Produces a "merged" caller whose body absorbs the
  callee's blocks (renumbered) directly.
- Static decision (NOT tier-up-driven). Decided once at codegen time.
- New module: src/ir_inline.rs. Public entry: `pub fn inline_module(m: &mut Module)`.
- Pipeline placement: after ir_lower, BEFORE ir_typer. The typer is per-fn and
  type-conservative; inlining first means the typer sees fused bodies and can
  elide more dispatch (composes with .11.24.4 arithmetic dispatch elision).
- v0 call-shape scope: "everything statically resolvable" (user decision
  2026-05-11) — TailCall, Call+Cont, TailCallClosure, CallClosure where the
  closure's MakeClosure is in the same block.
- v0 heuristic: leaf callees only, ≤ 8 IR stmts (user decision 2026-05-11).
  Single-callsite-anywhere heuristic is NOT in v0.

## Architecture (researched 2026-05-11 against actual IR)

fz-IR is in CPS form (see src/fz_ir.rs). Four callsite shapes exist:

  1. Term::TailCall { callee: FnId, args }
       Caller forwards its continuation; callee receives our cont_ptr.
       Runtime (ir_codegen::emit_tail_call, codegen.rs:2185): if
       self_id == callee_id, overwrite frame slots in place; else alloc fresh.

  2. Term::Call { callee: FnId, args, continuation: Cont { fn_id, captured } }
       Allocates a NEW continuation frame (emit_call, codegen.rs:2129), runs
       callee. Callee's Return writes result into cont_frame[24] (cont's first
       param slot) and returns cont_ptr; trampoline drives the continuation.

  3. Term::TailCallClosure { closure: Var, args }
       Closure value (Value::IrClosure { fn_id, captured }) is invoked. The
       lambda fn's entry params = [closure_captured..., args...].
       (See ir_interp.rs:154-166 and ir_lower.rs:586-592.)

  4. Term::CallClosure { closure: Var, args, continuation }
       Like (2) but for closures (emit_call_closure, codegen.rs:2224).

Continuations are themselves real FnIrs in the module — produced by
cps_split_call (ir_lower.rs:664) and cps_split_call_closure (ir_lower.rs:629).
A continuation's entry block params are [result_param, cap_params...] — result
FIRST, then captured.

Closures are produced by Prim::MakeClosure(FnId, captured_vars). The lambda's
entry params order is [captured..., callsite_args...].

## Inlining semantics by case

  Case 1 (TailCall):
    - Renumber callee's blocks and vars (alpha_rename).
    - Caller's `TailCall(F, args)` becomes `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` becomes caller's `Return(v')` (caller's cont unchanged).
    - F's `Halt(v)` becomes `Halt(v')`.

  Case 2 (Call+Cont):
    - Renumber.
    - Caller's `Call(F, args) -> Cont{c, captured}` becomes
      `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` becomes `TailCall(c, [v', captured...])`. The
      continuation is a real FnIr; calling it as TailCall is operationally
      identical to the runtime's cont mechanism (ir_interp.rs:128-134
      confirms).
    - F's `Halt(v)` becomes `Halt(v')`.

  Case 3 (TailCallClosure): scan block stmts for `Let(cv, MakeClosure(F,
    captured))` preceding the term, with cv not reassigned afterward. If
    found, rewrite term as `TailCall(F, [captured..., args...])`, then apply
    Case 1.

  Case 4 (CallClosure): same closure-trace; rewrite as `Call(F, [captured...,
    args...], cont)`, then apply Case 2.

## Why "leaf" is the right v0 heuristic

A leaf fn has only Goto/If/Return/Halt terminators — NO Call/TailCall/
CallClosure/TailCallClosure inside its body. Leaf-only inlining:

  - Cannot reintroduce recursion (cycles must traverse a Call/TailCall term).
    Direct self-recursion (callee.id == caller.id) is also defended against.
  - Doesn't pull in dependent continuations or nested closures.
  - Keeps frame schemas valid: ir_codegen builds schemas from each fn's entry
    param count (codegen.rs:1473-1480). Inlining doesn't change the caller's
    entry params, so the caller's schema is unaffected. The callee's schema
    is also untouched; if other callsites remain, the callee is still
    compiled normally.
  - The introduced post-inline Return→TailCall (Case 2) adds a new TailCall
    to the caller, but to an already-existing continuation FnIr — no new fns
    are created, no schemas change.

## Frame schema interaction

ir_liveness::analyze_module (src/ir_liveness.rs) computes "carried across
calls" frame slots but is NOT currently wired into codegen. ir_codegen builds
its own schemas from entry-block param count and does NOT consult
FnIr.frame_schema_id. Therefore the inliner does NOT need to recompute frame
schemas. (Wiring ir_liveness in is orthogonal — see fz-ul4.11.29.)

## Configuration

  pub const INLINE_BUDGET: usize = 8;     // total Stmt::Let cap on callee
  pub const MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 3;    // fixpoint cap
  pub const GROWTH_CAP: usize = 4;        // per-fn post-inline ≤ 4× original

User-decided 2026-05-11. Consts in ir_inline.rs, not runtime knobs.

## Children (in dependency order)

  - fz-ul4.11.15.1 — Inliner skeleton + leaf detector + alpha-rename primitives
  - fz-ul4.11.15.2 — TailCall inlining (Case 1)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.3 — Call+Cont inlining (Case 2)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.4 — Closure-call inlining (Cases 3-4)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.5 — Fixpoint + bloat cap + pipeline integration
  - fz-ul4.11.15.6 — Hot-loop assert + SAMPLE parity + ticket closeout

## Tests (rolled up across children)

  - After inlining, count(100000, 0) doesn't allocate per-call (tail-recursion
    + single merged frame — .11.8 tail-call frame reuse already handles the
    same-schema case; inlining removes call overhead in non-self-recursive
    chains).
  - Hot loops show no per-iter allocation.
  - GC still finds correct roots in inlined code (defer to .11.15.6 if no
    GC test hooks; otherwise allocate inside hot loop, force GC, verify).
  - Inliner doesn't introduce code bloat beyond GROWTH_CAP per fn.
  - ir_interp parity: for a property-test suite, inlined module produces
    same values as un-inlined module.

## Risks

  - Inliner correctness: must not violate liveness invariants or frame
    layouts. Mitigation: ir_interp parity test on every change.
  - Inliner too aggressive: code bloat → instruction cache pressure.
    Mitigation: GROWTH_CAP + MAX_ITERATIONS.
  - Forgetting to remap a nested Var (e.g., BitFieldIr::size = BitSizeIr::
    Var(v) inside MakeBitstring/BitReadField). Mitigation: exhaustive Prim
    walk in alpha_rename; targeted test per Prim variant.

## Deferred (tracked as explicit follow-up tickets)

  - fz-ul4.11.26 — @no_inline attribute (per-fn opt-out)
  - fz-ul4.11.27 — Cross-module inlining
  - fz-ul4.11.28 — Bounded recursion unrolling
  - fz-ul4.11.29 — Dead-callee DCE pass
  - fz-ul4.11.30 — Wire ir_liveness into codegen (orthogonal cleanup; currently
    dead code)

## References

  - src/fz_ir.rs            — IR types: Term (220-246), Prim (155-205), Cont
                              (213-218), FnIr (256-264), Module (272-290)
  - src/ir_lower.rs         — cps_split_call (664), cps_split_call_closure (629),
                              MakeClosure construction (574-627)
  - src/ir_codegen.rs       — emit_call (2129), emit_tail_call (2185),
                              emit_call_closure (2224), schema build (1470-1480)
  - src/ir_interp.rs        — call/return semantics (128-180)
  - src/ir_liveness.rs      — not currently wired into codegen (see .11.30)
  - src/ir_typer.rs         — runs AFTER inliner in the planned pipeline
title: Frame inliner (performance optimization)
